title: When employees leave, their AI clones carry on working
tldr: Chinese companies are building AI clones of departed employees to retain their knowledge and keep producing work. A provocative new frontier where employee-generated content meets synthetic media.
angle: What 'AI employee clones' mean for institutional knowledge, content governance, and the human voice in corporate comms.

title: The Creator Economy's Biggest Winners May Never Go Viral
tldr: Argues the durable creator economy winners are niche, consistent creators monetizing trust rather than chasing virality. A useful reframe on what sustainable audience-building actually looks like.
angle: Why 'boring' niche creators beat viral ones — and what that teaches brands about consistency over reach.

title: Why your creative eye is the most valuable thing in the studio right now
tldr: As anyone can generate content instantly, taste and judgment become the scarce, valuable asset. A human-over-polish argument for the AI era.
angle: In an age of infinite AI output, curation and taste are the new craft — how content teams should reorganize around judgment.

title: Social networks, online video outweigh traditional media in 2026
tldr: New data shows social and online video have decisively overtaken traditional media in consumption. A key shift for anyone planning content distribution.
angle: The tipping point is here: what brands should stop funding now that social video beats legacy media.

title: Forecasting the future: how can publishers thrive in the AI search era?
tldr: The traffic model that funded a decade of digital publishing is being dismantled by zero-click AI search, yet most publishers still optimize for it. Concrete reframe on survival strategies.
angle: Building a content strategy that survives zero-click: direct audience, owned channels, and value beyond traffic.

title: Why employee advocacy matters in 2026 + what actually works
tldr: Practical look at employee advocacy and EGC: what genuinely drives reach and trust versus what fails. Directly relevant to Igor's interest in employee-generated content.
angle: Employee advocacy that doesn't feel forced: how to build EGC programs people actually want to join.

title: Coach unites celebrities, Gen Z storytelling under new brand platform
tldr: Coach launches a brand platform built around Gen Z storytelling and celebrity, signaling how heritage brands court younger audiences. A concrete campaign breakdown.
why_fresh: routine campaign launch but useful case
angle: How a legacy brand translates heritage into Gen Z narrative — a case study in brand repositioning.

title: Pulling back the curtain on Nissan's 'follow the sun' global comms structure
tldr: Nissan restructured global comms around a centralized center of excellence and a follow-the-sun model to speed response and cut duplication. A rare operational look at comms ops at scale.
angle: Designing a 24-hour global comms operating model: lessons from Nissan's center-of-excellence approach.

title: My Job Changed and Nobody Told Me: What AI Visibility Means for Marketers
tldr: AI visibility (being cited by answer engines) has quietly redefined the comms job across paid, earned, owned and shared media. A clear-eyed look at the new mandate.
angle: AI visibility is the new share of voice — how comms teams should retool measurement and content for answer engines.

title: How an Alaska news nonprofit bought the local newspaper it was founded to compete with
tldr: A nonprofit newsroom acquired the legacy local paper it once competed with, a notable model for local news consolidation and sustainability.
angle: When the disruptor buys the incumbent: what nonprofit news consolidation tells us about media business models.

title: Elise Zeng on Building More Authentic UGC Campaigns
tldr: A creator-marketing leader on redefining brand-creator collaboration toward more authentic user-generated content. Practical perspective on humanity over polish in campaigns.
angle: What makes UGC feel authentic vs. staged — a framework for brand-creator collaboration.

title: The Operating Model Behind Trustworthy Content at Scale
tldr: How to spot when a high-volume content program produces no real impact, with symptoms like competitors winning answer boxes and compliance flagging freelance work. A diagnostic for content ops.
angle: Volume isn't impact: building a content operating model that earns trust and answer-engine citations.

title: Media Consumption Trend to Watch: Podcasts Poised for Summertime Growth
tldr: CivicScience data shows more Americans tuning into podcasts, with a seasonal summer uptick. Useful signal for audio content planning.
angle: Audio's quiet summer surge: what podcast consumption data means for B2B content planning.

title: How to use AI doom marketing to dupe the media and rake in billions in 10 easy steps
tldr: A sharp satirical critique of how AI companies weaponize doom narratives to drive coverage and valuations. Smart media-literacy fuel for anyone covering AI.
angle: How 'AI doom' became a PR strategy — and how communicators should read the narrative economics behind it.
